research.summary: Roofing is storm-season-driven — rankings BEFORE a storm capture surge demand the moment hail/wind hits. Companies that wait until 'roof repair near me' searches spike are too late. Strategy: rank steadily year-round so when storm season triggers query surges, you're already at the top. Competitive roofing markets typically need 50+ recent reviews to break into the top 3 Map Pack. Review recency matters as much as count.
research.recommendedPrimaryCategoryReasoning: Empirically dominant in Culver City top-5 (10/10 use 'Roofing contractor'). Canonical and unambiguous.

topRankingFactors.benchmark: All fields filled. Primary = Roofing contractor. Service attributes complete.
50+ recent reviews needed to break top 3 in competitive markets. Recent stream beats lifetime total.
Real project photos build conversion. Stale stock images hurt.
Hail-prep / storm-prep content 30-60 days before season. Posts weekly.
Mention 'we work with all major insurance carriers' + claim assistance.
State contractor license + bond info visible on GBP + website footer.
topRankingFactors.source: Scorpion Complete Guide to Roofing SEO

commonPitfalls: No before/after photos (top conversion signal missing)
Generic stock roof imagery instead of real projects
No mention of insurance claim handling (high-value differentiator)
Stale GBP posts during storm season (when search demand spikes)
Single-page website with no per-city landing pages
License number not visible (trust signal weakened)
research.titleTagExample: Roofing Contractor in Culver City, CA | Apex Roofing
research.h1Example: Licensed Roofing Contractor in Culver City — 25-Year Warranty
research.metaDescPattern: Service + city + differentiator (license, warranty, response time, insurance claims)
research.metaDescExample: Licensed & insured roofing contractor in Culver City, CA. Hail damage repair, insurance claim assistance, free estimates, 25-yr warranty. Call (310) 555-0300.
voiceoverHooks: Review count + recency gap vs top 3 (50+ recent threshold)
Stale GBP posts (especially during storm season)
License/bond not visible on GBP description
voiceoverAntipatterns: DO NOT suggest stock roof imagery — top performers use real project photos.
DO NOT advise broader categories — 'Roofing contractor' is the empirical winner.
DO NOT cite review count without recency context.
clientOnboardingChecklist: Add 4 recommended secondary categories where services match
Fill all 14 service attributes including insurance + emergency
Display license + bond info on GBP description + website footer
Upload 20+ before/after photos of real projects (not stock)
Set up SMS post-job review request (24-48h delay, direct GBP link)
Build citation profile across 15 targets including manufacturer directories
Add LocalBusiness schema with NAP + service area + license
Establish weekly GBP post cadence — schedule seasonal content 30-60 days ahead
Set up review response process — target ≥85% response rate
Build per-city service area landing pages (min 4 for multi-city operators)
Audit website: HTTPS, schema, <2.5s load, NAP above fold, insurance language
Add 'work with all major insurance carriers' messaging prominently

kpiTargets.rankingTimeline: Map Pack 30-60 days; meaningful lead lift 3-5 months

industryNotes: Storm season triggers 5-10x normal search volume — ranking established 30-60 days BEFORE captures the wave.
Insurance claim handling is the highest-converting differentiator in roofing.
GAF Master Elite + Owens Corning Platinum Preferred certifications open additional directory citations + trust signals.
Before/after photos consistently rank as top GBP conversion lever.
consumesIn: FUTURE: Brain client-strategy module — roofing clients need seasonal content calendar planning
FUTURE: admin onboarding wizard with manufacturer-certification check
FUTURE: portal KPI dashboard with kpiTargets + storm-readiness gauge
